metadata.title: The Ghost In The Machine Why Ai Is
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Nirali Bhansali
tags: The Ghost In The Machine Why Ai Is
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nirali Bhansali — Humanitarians AI Fellow. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. We've entered the age of AEO, Answer Engine Optimization, changing how writing gets surfaced. Answer engines don't want generic content; they want authority, nuance, and a human soul. A practical move is opening articles with a Direct Answer block. The old fear that AI would replace writers misses that AI now rewards distinctly human authority.

artifactLines: We've entered the age of AEO, Answer Engine Optimization, changing how writing gets surfaced.
Answer engines don't want generic content; they want authority, nuance, and a human soul.
A practical move is opening articles with a Direct Answer block.
The old fear that AI would replace writers misses that AI now rewards distinctly human authority.

beats.narration_text: The shift to AEO, where answer engines want authority and human soul instead of generic content, changed how I think about publishing. The concrete tactic was starting with a 'Direct Answer' block. Take one of my existing articles and (1) write the Direct Answer block that opens it for answer engines, (2) identify where it currently reads as generic content versus genuine authority, and (3) add the nuance and human specificity that would make an AI cite me rather than replace me.
